theme_and_archetype.vibe: Physical ledger book, tactile, trustworthy, grounded, focused. Avoids sterile tech 'SaaS' looks in favor of a modern analog accounting feel.

scales.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-medium tracking-tight text-[#1C1917]

layout_and_spacing.strategy: Generous spacing. The UI should breathe like a well-margined physical book.
layout_and_spacing.bento_grid: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6 lg:gap-8
layout_and_spacing.cards: bg-white border border-[#E7E5E4] rounded-sm shadow-sm p-6 sm:p-8

split_view_ledger.description: The signature visual moment mimicking a physical open ledger book.
split_view_ledger.structure: A wide container split 50/50. Left side is 'Money Coming In' (Collections), Right side is 'Money Going Out' (Distributions).
split_view_ledger.divider: A vertical center divider `border-l-2 border-[#D6D3D1] border-dashed` to represent the book binding or physical separation.
split_view_ledger.row_style: Each entry should be a clean flex row. Date on left (small, secondary), Amount on right (mono, bold). Use 1px solid bottom border for rows to mimic lined paper.
split_view_ledger.hover_effect: Hovering a row gives a subtle background shift to track the eye across the ledger without breaking the analog feel.

calculate_module.interaction: User clicks 'Calculate' button -> Confirmation Modal ('Calculate totals for current view?') -> Instantly reveals a prominent sticky or modal view showing Total Incoming, Total Outgoing, and Net Balance.
calculate_module.visual: Net balance should be massive (`text-5xl font-serif`) to give emotional weight to the calculation.

add_payment_form.style: Should feel like writing an entry into a book. Prefer input variants with bottom-borders (`border-b border-[#D6D3D1] rounded-none bg-transparent`) over heavy filled boxes. Toggle between In/Out with clear color shifts (Emerald vs Orange).

daily_log_and_calendar.style: Two main lists side-by-side or stacked cleanly: 'Payment Given Today' and 'Payment Given Yesterday'. Use the interactive calendar component alongside it to pick historical dates. Calendar should use serif headers and mono numbers.

shadcn.card: Remove large border-radius. Use `rounded-sm`. Change default shadow to a very soft ambient shadow `shadow-[0_4px_20px_-4px_rgba(0,0,0,0.05)]`.
shadcn.button: Use flat colors, no gradients. Primary buttons should be `bg-[#292524] text-white hover:bg-[#1C1917]`. Cancel/Secondary: `bg-transparent border border-[#D6D3D1] text-[#292524] hover:bg-[#F9F8F6]`.
shadcn.input: Remove standard focus rings. Use `focus-visible:ring-1 focus-visible:ring-[#78716C] focus-visible:border-[#78716C]`.
shadcn.dialog: Backdrop should be `backdrop-blur-md bg-[#1C1917]/30`. Dialog panel should be `bg-[#F9F8F6] border border-[#E7E5E4] rounded-sm`.
shadcn.calendar: Override default rounded corners to `rounded-sm`. Apply `font-serif` to month/year header and `font-mono` to dates.

icons_and_media.icon_style: Use stroke-width 1.5 for a delicate, refined look matching the serif typography.

login_background.description: Minimalist paper texture. Use this as a subtle overlay (`opacity-30 mix-blend-multiply`) on the login screen or behind the ledger book component to add tactile warmth.

login_hero.description: Abstract minimalist workspace. Use for the left half of a split-screen login page to establish a calm, focused environment for the accountant.

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Strict adherence to contrast ratios. Emerald-800 and Orange-800 provide excellent contrast against white/stone-50.
accessibility_and_testing.currency_symbol: Always use '₹' correctly formatted with the Indian numbering system (e.g., ₹ 1,50,000).

security.logic: Single accountant login. JWT-based custom auth. UI must inherently hide or disable edit/delete buttons for past entries to reflect the immutable ledger requirement.
